title: Cohen & Steers REIT and Preferred and Income Fund, Inc. (RNP) Notification of Sources of Distribution Under Section 19(a)
author: Prnewswire
description: Cohen & Steers REIT and Preferred and Income Fund announced its May 2026 distribution details under its managed distribution policy. The fund's May distribution of $0.1360 per share consists of net investment income (38.82%), long-term capital gains (53.82%), and return of capital (7.36%). Year-to-date through May 31, 2026, cumulative distributions total $0.6800 per share. The fund's year-to-date cumulative total return is 19.61% while the cumulative distribution rate is 23.09%, indicating distributions exceed current investment performance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the fund manager, Cohen & Steers is executing its stated managed distribution policy as designed. The announcement is routine regulatory disclosure with no indication of operational issues or performance concerns, warranting a neutral sentiment.

title: Cohen & Steers Closed-End Opportunity Fund, Inc. (FOF) Notification of Sources of Distribution Under Section 19(a)
author: Prnewswire
description: Cohen & Steers Closed-End Opportunity Fund announced its May 2026 distribution of $0.0870 per share, comprised of 38.39% net investment income and 61.61% net realized long-term capital gains. Year-to-date through May 31, 2026, cumulative distributions total $0.4350 per share. The Fund's year-to-date cumulative total return is 16.22% while the cumulative distribution rate is 23.18%, and the five-year average annual total return is 38.80%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The parent company is mentioned only as the fund manager. The article contains no information about Cohen & Steers Inc's operations, financial performance, or business developments that would warrant a positive or negative sentiment rating.

title: Cohen & Steers Quality Income Realty Fund, Inc. (RQI) Notification of Sources of Distribution Under Section 19(a)
author: Prnewswire
description: Cohen & Steers Quality Income Realty Fund announced its May 2026 distribution details under its managed distribution policy. The May distribution of $0.09 per share consists entirely of net realized long-term capital gains. Year-to-date through May 31, 2026, cumulative distributions total $0.45 per share, also 100% from long-term capital gains. The fund reported a year-to-date cumulative total return of 114.66% with a 23.34% cumulative distribution rate.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company managing the RQI fund, Cohen & Steers is mentioned in context of the fund's operations and distribution policy. While the fund's strong performance is positive, the article provides no direct information about the parent company's overall business performance or financial condition.

title: Cohen & Steers Infrastructure Fund, Inc. (UTF) Notification of Sources of Distribution Under Section 19(a)
author: Prnewswire
description: Cohen & Steers Infrastructure Fund announced its May 2026 distribution of $0.1650 per share, with year-to-date distributions of $0.7950 per share. The distribution includes net investment income, capital gains, and return of capital. The Fund reported a year-to-date cumulative total return of 116.71% and a five-year average annual total return of 39.64%, though distributions exceed income and capital gains, indicating partial return of capital.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the fund manager, Cohen & Steers is successfully executing its investment strategy with strong returns and maintaining a disciplined managed distribution policy. The firm's ability to realize long-term capital gains and deliver consistent distributions reflects effective asset management.

title: Cohen & Steers Announces Changes to Realty Indexes
author: Prnewswire
description: Cohen & Steers announced pending changes to its Global Realty Majors Portfolio Index (GRM) and International Realty Majors Portfolio Index (IRP), effective May 15, 2026. Azrieli Group Ltd. will be added to both indexes, while LEG Immobilien AG will be removed from GRM and Grainger Trust Plc will be removed from IRP.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement is a routine index rebalancing with no indication of positive or negative performance implications for the company itself.
Addition to both major Cohen & Steers indexes indicates recognition as a leading real estate company meeting the firm's qualitative screening criteria.
Removal from the GRM index suggests it no longer meets Cohen & Steers' criteria for leading global real estate securitization.
